proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 5.089 on regorafenib (n=10,022) vs 4.117 off (n=39,978); difference +0.972 months, Welch t-test p=1.7e-216.
Mean pfs_months 4.285 on cetuximab (n=15,078) vs 4.323 off; difference -0.038 months, p=0.088 — no benefit.
Mean pfs_months 4.301 on bevacizumab vs 4.320 off; diff -0.019 months, p=0.354 — null.
Mean pfs_months 4.316 on pembrolizumab vs 4.311 off; diff +0.005 months, p=0.856 — null.
Mean pfs_months 4.316 on encorafenib vs 4.311 off; diff +0.005 months, p=0.872 — null.
Mean pfs_months 4.271 on trastuzumab/tucatinib vs 4.315 off; diff -0.044 months, p=0.244 — null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Stage IV (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
Right-sided primary (right_sided_primary=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
kras_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0 patients.
braf_v600e=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0 patients.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
msi_high=1 patients differ in mean pfs_months vs msi_high=0.
her2_amplified=1 patients differ in mean pfs_months vs her2_amplified=0.
nras_mutation=1 patients differ in mean pfs_months vs nras_mutation=0.

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive correlation r=+0.766, p<1e-300. Each year of age associated with ~+0.18 mo PFS in adjusted OLS.
Strong negative correlation r=-0.352, p<1e-300. Adjusted OLS slope -1.17 mo per ECOG point.
Mean PFS 3.703 in stage IV vs 5.055 in non-stage IV; diff -1.352 mo, p<1e-300. Adjusted coef -1.36 mo.
Mean PFS 4.114 right-sided vs 4.419 left-sided; diff -0.305 mo, p=1.0e-49. Adjusted coef -0.29 mo.
Mean PFS 4.121 KRAS-mut vs 4.448 KRAS-WT; diff -0.327 mo, p=1.9e-58. Adjusted coef -0.35 mo.
Mean PFS 4.094 BRAF-V600E vs 4.322 WT; diff -0.228 mo, p=3.5e-7. Adjusted coef -0.36 mo.
Negative correlation r=-0.038, p=3e-17. Adjusted coef -0.005 mo per ng/mL CEA.
Positive correlation r=+0.102, p<1e-100. Adjusted coef +0.47 mo per g/dL albumin.
Negative correlation r=-0.123, p<1e-160. Adjusted coef -0.076 mo per percent weight loss.
Weak negative correlation r=-0.011, p=0.013; adjusted coef -0.0004 mo per U/L (significant in adjusted model).
Mean PFS 4.293 in MSI-high vs 4.313 in MSI-stable; diff -0.019, p=0.68 — null.
Mean PFS 4.258 HER2-amp vs 4.313 non-amp; diff -0.056, p=0.36 — null.
Mean PFS 4.526 NRAS-mut vs 4.305 WT; diff +0.221, p=7e-4 (univariate); adjusted coef +0.020, p=0.37 (NS adjusted).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model with all features and treatments, treatment_regorafenib has an independent positive effect on pfs_months.

In the same multivariable model, none of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has a non-zero independent main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib coefficient +0.943 months (95% CI 0.92, 0.96), t=97.86, p<1e-300. Confirms strong independent main effect.

Adjusted coefficients: cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19), bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73), pembrolizumab +0.000 (p=0.98), encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.63), trastuzumab/tucatinib +0.026 (p=0.07). All not significant — consistent with H3.2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab has a larger positive PFS effect in kras_mutation=0 patients than in kras_mutation=1 patients (interaction kras x cetuximab).
Cetuximab benefit is larger in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) than right-sided (right_sided_primary=1) tumors.
Pembrolizumab has a larger positive PFS effect in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients.
Encorafenib has a larger positive PFS effect in braf_v600e=1 patients than in braf_v600e=0 patients.
Trastuzumab/tucatinib has a larger positive PFS effect in her2_amplified=1 than her2_amplified=0 patients.
Regorafenib effect on PFS is modified by stage_iv (stronger in metastatic vs non-metastatic).
Regorafenib effect on PFS is modified by ecog_ps.

analyses.code: OLS with kras*cetuximab interaction adjusted for covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.024, p=0.19 — NOT significant. Stratified: KRAS=0 cetuximab effect -0.048; KRAS=1 effect -0.028. No KRAS modification.
Interaction coef +0.026, p=0.18 — not significant. Cetuximab effect ~ -0.05 (left) and -0.02 (right). No sidedness modification.
Interaction coef +0.059, p=0.27 — NOT significant. Stratified: MSI-high pembrolizumab effect +0.007 (n=396 vs 2117); MSI-stable +0.005. Hypothesis refuted.
Interaction coef +0.011, p=0.87. Stratified: BRAF-V600E encorafenib effect -0.129 (n=242 vs 2030, p=0.33); BRAF-WT effect +0.013. Hypothesis refuted (no benefit, slight numerical harm in BRAF-mut).
Interaction coef +0.015, p=0.87. Stratified: HER2-amp trastuzumab effect +0.017 (n=120 vs 1384); HER2-non-amp -0.046. Hypothesis refuted.
Interaction coef +0.035, p=0.075 — borderline. Stratified: stage_iv=0 +0.94, stage_iv=1 +1.01. Effect is large in both strata; not meaningfully modified by stage.
Interaction coef -0.052, p=3e-4 — significant but small. Stratified: ECOG=0 +0.93, ECOG=1 +1.00. Effect is similar across ECOG; clinical magnitude minimal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in patients with kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided KRAS-WT).
Among kras_mutation=1 patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
Among right_sided_primary=1 patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
Bevacizumab harms PFS in braf_v600e=1 patients (negative subgroup effect).

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect = +2.520 mo (on 6.639 vs off 4.119, n=3763 vs 15090), p≈0. Strongly supported.
Regorafenib effect in KRAS-mut = +0.013 mo (left+right combined); KRAS=1, right=0 +0.061 (p=0.17); KRAS=1, right=1 -0.04 (p=0.52). Supports no benefit in KRAS-mut.
Regorafenib effect in right-sided ≈ 0 (KRAS=0,right=1 +0.071 p=0.16; KRAS=1,right=1 -0.04 p=0.52). Supports no benefit in right-sided.

Bevacizumab effect in BRAF-V600E: 3.958 on (n=969) vs 4.195 off (n=1303); diff -0.237 mo, p=6.4e-3 (univariate). Adjusted bev*BRAF interaction p=0.49, suggesting confounding rather than independent harm.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding braf_v600e=0 to the regorafenib subgroup further refines (or preserves) the benefit; presence of braf_v600e=1 abolishes the regorafenib benefit even in left-sided KRAS-WT patients.
Regorafenib benefits patients with nras_mutation=1 even when KRAS is wild-type and primary is left-sided.

analyses.code: Stratified analysis on triple-WT and BRAF-mut left-sided KRAS-WT
analyses.result_summary: Left+KRAS=0+BRAF=0: regorafenib +1.807 mo (n=5035 vs 20289), p≈3e-293. Left+KRAS=0+BRAF=1: regorafenib -0.156 mo (n=468 vs 1804), p=0.13. BRAF V600E abolishes the regorafenib benefit. Hypothesis supported.

analyses.code: Stratified analysis: left + KRAS-WT + NRAS=1 + BRAF-WT
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect = +2.906 mo (on 7.018 vs off 4.112, n=197 vs 732), p=7e-25. NRAS-mut patients still benefit when KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit requires the simultaneous combination right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0; nras_mutation status is not part of the qualifying subgroup.
Within the regorafenib responsive subgroup defined in h7.1, the magnitude of benefit is roughly constant regardless of stage_iv and ecog_ps.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with rego_subgroup = (right_sided_primary=0 & kras_mutation=0 & braf_v600e=0) and rego x rego_subgroup interaction
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib main effect outside subgroup: -0.003 mo (p=0.77). Interaction coef = +2.74 mo (p<1e-300). Stratified: outside subgroup +0.030 mo (p=0.29); inside +2.758 mo (p<1e-300). Subgroup definition fully captures the regorafenib effect.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests within rego_subgroup by stage_iv and ecog_ps
analyses.result_summary: ECOG=0 +2.665, ECOG=1 +2.765, ECOG=2 +2.743. stage_iv=0 +2.619, stage_iv=1 +2.859. Effect roughly constant; modest numerical differences. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Two-feature subgroup search (across all binary features and median-split continuous variables) will identify additional treatment x feature combinations with adjusted PFS benefits beyond regorafenib's subgroup.

analyses.code: Exhaustive 2-modifier subgroup t-tests for each treatment
analyses.result_summary: Top non-regorafenib signals were small in magnitude (|diff|<0.5 mo) and at most p=3e-3 (e.g., bevacizumab in right-sided BRAF-V600E -0.42 mo p=3e-3; cetuximab in old age + low weight loss -0.10 mo p=0.02). All are within multiple-testing noise given thousands of cells tested. No new robust subgroup effects discovered.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all features, every treatment x feature interaction other than regorafenib's interactions with right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and braf_v600e is non-significant.

analyses.code: OLS with one treatment x modifier interaction at a time, controlling for all features
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab: best p=0.13 (NRAS); bevacizumab best p=0.08 (MSI); pembrolizumab best p=0.23; encorafenib best p=0.08 (NRAS); trastuzumab/tuc best p=0.08 (KRAS). None reach 0.05. Regorafenib: right_sided_primary p<1e-300 (-1.46), kras_mutation p<1e-300 (-1.63), braf_v600e p=5e-104 (-0.99), nras_mutation p=1e-57 (+0.89), all robust modifiers. Hypothesis supported (mostly).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the canonical biomarker-positive subgroup (msi_high=1) plus an additional modifier, pembrolizumab improves PFS.
Within braf_v600e=1 plus an additional modifier, encorafenib improves PFS.
Within her2_amplified=1 plus an additional modifier, trastuzumab/tucatinib improves PFS.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-tests within msi_high=1 stratified by additional modifiers
analyses.result_summary: Best subgroup: msi_high=1 & hgb_low=0 +0.231 (p=0.22). All p>=0.21. Pembrolizumab provides no detectable PFS benefit even in MSI-high subgroups.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-tests within braf_v600e=1 stratified by additional modifiers
analyses.result_summary: Strongest signal: braf_v600e=1 & ecog_high=0 (ECOG=0): encorafenib effect -0.543 mo (n=82 vs 694, p=8e-3), i.e., possible NUMERICAL HARM rather than benefit. No supportive subgroup with positive effect identified.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-tests within her2_amplified=1 stratified by additional modifiers
analyses.result_summary: Best signals: stage_iv=0 -0.557 (p=0.06), stage_iv=1 +0.522 (p=0.07). Inconsistent direction and below significance. No subgroup benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the canonical anti-EGFR-eligible subgroup (KRAS-WT, NRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided), cetuximab improves PFS.
Three-modifier subgroup search reveals additional treatment effects for cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, or trastuzumab/tucatinib beyond what 2-modifier search found.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab effect: 4.617 on (n=5067) vs 4.684 off (n=11376); diff -0.067 mo, p=0.13. NUMERICAL HARM (small) — hypothesis refuted: cetuximab does NOT improve PFS even in canonical anti-EGFR-eligible patients.

analyses.code: Exhaustive 3-modifier subgroup t-tests for each non-regorafenib treatment
analyses.result_summary: Top non-regorafenib subgroups (all weak, multiple-testing-vulnerable): bevacizumab in right-sided + age_old + wl_high -0.18 mo (p=1.4e-4); bevacizumab in right-sided + BRAF-V600E + age_old -0.53 (p=6e-4); pembrolizumab in male + stage_iv + BRAF-V600E +0.70 (p=2e-3). None survive correction for thousands of subgroups tested. Hypothesis only weakly supported (no robust new subgroups).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final refined subgroup hypothesis: regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately 2.7 months in patients with right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0; outside this subgroup the regorafenib effect is null (~0 months).
Adjusted main treatment effects of cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib are essentially zero in this dataset, with no robust subgroups of benefit identified.

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS interaction model + stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup: +2.758 mo (n=3461 on / 13911 off, p<1e-300). Outside subgroup: +0.030 mo (n=6561 on / 26067 off, p=0.29). Adjusted interaction coefficient +2.74 mo, p<1e-300. ECOG and stage_iv do not further modify; NRAS does not. Hypothesis strongly supported.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main effect for each non-regorafenib treatment with all covariates
analyses.result_summary: cetuximab -0.013 (p=0.16); bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.70); pembrolizumab +0.000 (p=0.99); encorafenib -0.001 (p=0.93); trastuzumab/tuc +0.024 (p=0.12). All within noise. Hypothesis supported: no clinically meaningful PFS benefit from these five treatments at the population level or in mechanistically motivated subgroups.
